generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE contracts.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) match the expected expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` helpers to define reusable expressions within AGREE contracts.
Range checks often use the pattern: `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or dependencies not present in the AADL model.
Avoid adding architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on fixing the contract itself.
Do not attempt to use subcomponent paths directly within component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction details (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, owners)
